title: Director Sells 20,000 Shares of Financial Services Provider, According to Latest SEC Filing
author: Jake Lerch
description: Director Rhodes R Bobbitt of Hilltop Holdings sold 20,000 shares worth approximately $758,000 on May 26-27, 2026. The sale represented 17.09% of his direct holdings, continuing a pattern of regular insider selling. While the stock has outperformed the S&P 500 over the past year (up 32.5%), its long-term performance lags significantly behind the broader market.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The insider sale is part of a consistent historical pattern rather than indicating a sudden change in disposition. The stock appears fairly valued at 14.5x P/E (below market average), offers a 2.1% dividend yield, and has beaten the S&P 500 over the past year (+32.5% vs +29.6%). However, long-term underperformance (14% over 5 years vs 94% for S&P 500) and the director's continued selling activity suggest neither strong bullish nor bearish signals.

title: This Regional Bank's Chief Accounting Officer Just Sold 2,000 Shares for $76,000
author: Sarah Sidlow
description: Keith Bornemann, Chief Accounting Officer at Hilltop Holdings Inc., sold 2,000 shares for $76,000 on May 5, 2026, reducing his direct ownership by 20.18%. The sale aligns with his historical trading patterns and reflects a gradual reduction in his stake over time. Hilltop Holdings reported strong Q1 2026 results with earnings beating consensus estimates, though the regional banking sector remains volatile.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The ETF has returned 8.5% since January 1, 2026, underperforming Hilltop Holdings' 11.26% return. The regional banking sector remains complex and risky due to concentration risk and historical crises, though it shows modest positive performance year-to-date.

The insider sale is routine and consistent with historical patterns, showing controlled liquidity management rather than a loss of confidence. Q1 2026 earnings beat consensus estimates with strong net interest income and lower credit loss provisions. However, the regional banking sector remains inherently volatile and risky, with the stock up 11.26% YTD but facing sector-wide challenges.

title: Hilltop Holdings' Chief Accounting Officer Sells 2,200 Shares for $77,000. Should Investors Be Concerned?
author: Isac Simon
description: Hilltop Holdings' Chief Accounting Officer sold 2,200 shares, representing 20.2% of his holdings. While the company has shown recent revenue and profit improvements, challenges remain in the housing market and mortgage origination.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed financial performance with recent revenue and profit improvements, but facing potential challenges from low home sales and reduced mortgage origination volumes

title: Hilltop Holdings (HTH) Weak Asset Quality, Mortgage Volume Hurt
author: Zacks Equity Research
description: Hilltop Holdings' (HTH) deteriorating asset quality, low mortgage origination volumes and higher expense base are headwinds. High rates and a sound balance sheet offer some relief.

title: Why Hilltop Holdings Stock Is Up Today
author: newsfeedback@fool.com (Lou Whiteman)
description: The bank's diverse business was able to offset continued mortgage weakness.
